🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Cranshaws Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* borders
*County:* Scottish Borders
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 55.8484, -2.5094
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cranshaws_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Cranshaws%20Castle%20Scottish%20Borders%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/cranshaws-castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 8 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* needs more research. no google maps listing seems to exist. looks like you could stay there at one point but not sure if this is still true. https://www.gentlemansbutler.com/cranshaws-castle-luxurious-scottish-getaway/

*Description:*
Cranshaws Castle or Cranshaws Tower is a privately owned 15th-century pele situated by the village of Cranshaws in Berwickshire, Scotland. The building is still in use as a residence, and is protected as a category A listed building.

_Listing 1 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Castle Craig
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* borders
*County:* Scottish Borders
*Type:* castle
*Period:* Medieval
*Coords:* 41.5565, -72.8354
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Castlecraig
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Castle%20Craig%20Scottish%20Borders%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/castle-craig.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* Delete associated google maps link. Needs more research. No google places seem to exist. 

*Description:*
Castle Craig, located on East Peak in the Hanging Hills, is constructed of trap rock and is 32 feet (10 m) in height and has a base 58 feet (18 m) in circumference. A metal interior stairway is used to ascend the observation deck on top. It stands 976 feet (297 m) above sea level and provides an expansive view of the greater Meriden, Connecticut area.

_Listing 2 of 10_
